fields.description:
## What
Read-only derivations over git history + the working tree for the code-health metrics that are durably git-derivable **under the current module-size mechanism**: module-size distribution against the hard cap (module count, count within N lines of the cap, largest modules), cap-change events, short-term churn, and the refactor-to-addition ratio.

## Current mechanism (the plan is grounded in this, not the deleted ratchet)
The old per-file ceilings ratchet is **gone**: `.github/module-size-ceilings.txt` and `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt` were deleted (epic 716f "delete the drained allowlist mechanism"). Module size is now a **single, flat, admin-locked hard cap** in `.github/module-size-limit.txt` (currently `800`); CI fails if ANY `src/rebar` file exceeds it. So the metrics derive from (a) scanning `src/rebar/**/*.py` line counts against that cap, and (b) `git log`. There is **no** "allowlisted-module count", "total ceiling LOC", or "new vs raised ceilings" to read â€” those data sources no longer exist.

## Files
- `src/rebar/metrics/git_metrics.py` â€” new: read-only functions, registered into `src/rebar/metrics/registry.py`. Provide:
  - `module_size_distribution(repo_root, ref="HEAD")` â€” parse `src/rebar/**/*.py` LOC (via `git ls-tree`/`git show` at the ref, or a tree walk) â†’ `{count, near_cap_count (within 10% of the 800 cap), max_loc, over_cap_count}`. The cap is read from `.github/module-size-limit.txt` (single-sourced, not hardcoded).
  - `cap_change_events(repo_root, since, until)` â€” `git log` over `.github/module-size-limit.txt` in range â†’ list of `(commit, old_limit, new_limit)` (the cap is admin-locked, so this is rare but real signal).
  - `churn(repo_root, since, until)` and `refactor_to_addition_ratio(repo_root, since, until)` â€” `git log --numstat` over the range â†’ summed insertions/deletions and the deletions:insertions ratio.
- `tests/unit/metrics/test_git_metrics.py` â€” new.

## Approach
Everything is deterministic and read-only. Distribution scans the tree at a ref against the single-sourced cap (no deleted files parsed); churn/refactor come from `git log --numstat`; cap changes from the limit file's history. Registered into the c085 registry. Per the c085 contract, `evaluate()` yields `Unavailable` ONLY when a metric's `compute` callable returns `None`; so each git-metric `compute` returns `None` (never `[]`/`{}`/`0`) when its range yields no commits or the source file is absent, and distinguishes that "no data accrued" case from a genuine measured zero (a real churn of 0 over a populated range returns `0`, not `None`). `source="git"`, `confidence="high"` (authoritative). Nothing is written.

## Tests
- Unit (real temp git repo): a fixture tree with modules of known sizes yields the expected `module_size_distribution` (count, max_loc, near_cap_count against a fixture cap file); a `git log --numstat` fixture over a range yields the expected churn totals and refactor:addition ratio; a commit that changes `.github/module-size-limit.txt` is surfaced by `cap_change_events`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `tests/unit/metrics/test_git_metrics.py` passes against a temp git repo: `module_size_distribution` over a fixture tree returns `max_loc` equal to the largest fixture module's line count and `count` equal to the number of `.py` modules scanned (exact-value asserts).
- [ ] `refactor_to_addition_ratio` over a two-commit numstat fixture (one add-heavy, one delete-heavy) returns the exact deletions/insertions ratio â€” asserted numerically.
- [ ] `churn` over a two-commit numstat fixture returns the exact summed insertions and deletions across the range (e.g. `{"insertions": 100, "deletions": 40}` for a 100-add then 40-delete fixture) â€” asserted numerically.
- [ ] a commit changing `.github/module-size-limit.txt` is reported by `cap_change_events` with the old and new limit values; a range with no such commit returns an empty list (both asserted).
- [ ] the new metric ids actually appear in `REGISTRY` â€” assert that a `MetricSpec` with each of `module_size_distribution`, `churn`, `cap_change_events`, `refactor_to_addition_ratio` is present (iterate `REGISTRY` and check `.id`), each carrying `source="git"` â€” so the assertion fails if a metric is not registered (not merely that `is_authoritative("git")` is trivially True).
